name: Pod Debug with Ephemeral Containers
description: Debug running pods using ephemeral debug containers, especially useful for distroless images that lack debugging tools.
trendingReason: Ephemeral containers are now GA and essential for debugging minimal images

steps.title: Attach an ephemeral debug container to a running pod
steps.description: Add a temporary debug container with a full toolkit to an existing pod without restarting it.
steps.tip: The --target flag shares the process namespace with the specified container so you can see its processes.

steps.title: Debug with a copy of the pod
steps.description: Create a copy of the pod with a debug container added, useful when you cannot modify the running pod.

steps.title: Debug a node directly
steps.description: Create a privileged pod on a specific node for host-level debugging.
steps.warning: This creates a privileged pod with host filesystem access at /host. Use with caution.

steps.title: Inspect pod events and conditions
steps.description: View events and conditions to understand why a pod is failing.

steps.title: Clean up debug pods
steps.description: Remove any debug pod copies you created.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Pod Debug Workflow
set -euo pipefail

POD=${1:?"Usage: $0 <pod-name> [namespace]"}
NS=${2:-"default"}

echo "=== Pod Status ==="
kubectl get pod "$POD" -n "$NS" -o wide

echo ""
echo "=== Pod Events ==="
kubectl get events -n "$NS" --field-selector "involvedObject.name=$POD" --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10

echo ""
echo "=== Container Statuses ==="
kubectl get pod "$POD" -n "$NS" -o jsonpath='{range .status.containerStatuses[*]}{.name}: {.state}{"
"}{end}'

echo ""
echo "=== Debug Commands ==="
echo "Ephemeral:  kubectl debug -it $POD -n $NS --image=busybox --target=<container>"
echo "Pod copy:   kubectl debug $POD -n $NS -it --image=nicolaka/netshoot --copy-to=debug-pod --share-processes"
echo "Logs:       kubectl logs $POD -n $NS --all-containers"

faq.question: What is the difference between debug and exec?
faq.answer: exec runs a command in an existing container that must have the binary. debug creates a new ephemeral container with its own image, so you can use any tools regardless of the original image.

faq.question: Do ephemeral containers persist after the pod restarts?
faq.answer: No. Ephemeral containers are temporary and cannot be restarted. If the pod restarts, you need to create a new debug container.

faq.question: Can I use ephemeral containers in production?
faq.answer: Yes, they are designed for production debugging. They do not affect the running containers and are removed when the session ends or the pod is deleted.
